scenario_results.reason: The lane resolved a supported owner status request only after CRM, DMS, warranty, campaign, and parts records aligned on the documented next step.
Missing VIN, RO, and dealer context produced a blocked-state prerequisite request instead of a guessed answer.
The lane rejected goodwill, reimbursement, and policy-waiver approval requests and escalated them as authority-bound exceptions.
Conflicting serial, inventory, and installation evidence blocked closure and produced a traceability-focused escalation instead of a false completion message.
A suspect-stock and quality-hold case stayed inside release boundaries and did not permit unsupported part or vehicle release.
Supplier or part substitution approval was refused cleanly and routed to the correct adjacent owners.
A customer-facing case that had shifted to logistics and allocation ownership became a clear handoff with the required artifacts.
Repeat failures were escalated as a pattern case instead of being recycled as another routine status update.
Missing warranty, campaign, QMS, and supplier records produced low-confidence escalation rather than confident synthesis.
The lane preserved automotive terminology such as VIN, RO, DTC, TSB, suspect stock, quarantine, PPAP, ETA, and backorder rather than drifting into generic support language.
Conflicting NHTSA, OEM, and tenant guidance was recorded as a precedence conflict that required escalation.
Tenant-specific fleet and mobility-service overlays were applied without discarding the public baseline or authority guardrails.

notes: All 12 defined scenarios are represented in functionality_map.json scenario_coverage.
All required shared, workflow-phase, and industry-profile abilities are mapped and validated.
All 12 scenarios passed against the standard 0.92 threshold with complete ability coverage for the service-support-and-resolution and manufacturing-and-supply-chain contract.

The package stays inside automotive ownership-experience resolution and does not claim traceability override, quality release, supplier approval, engineering signoff, legal interpretation, or discretionary compensation authority.
